message:
ui: WebGL renderer toggle observable on VectorField

Iteration 10. The last unwired Storage runtime toggle. Flipping the
WebGL checkbox in the Storage card now changes the VectorField
canvas's renderer choice — observable via the `data-renderer`
attribute and the `window.__RULakeWebGL` global.

ui/src/components/components.jsx — VectorField
  - Reads window.__RULakeWebGL on mount (defaults to feature-detect:
    true if `WebGL2RenderingContext` exists, false otherwise).
  - Listens for `rulake:webgl-toggle` CustomEvent and re-renders when
    fired — the StorageSettingsCard dispatches this whenever the user
    flips the checkbox.
  - When WebGL2 is enabled and supported, probes a real WebGL2 context
    (separate offscreen canvas), runs a clear-pass against the brand
    background color (verifies the GL program lives), and tags the
    main canvas with `data-renderer="webgl2"`. Point + ring rendering
    stays on Canvas 2D (full WebGL point-sprite is the v0.2 polish
    ADR-006 §10 commits to).
  - When disabled or WebGL2 unavailable: tags `data-renderer="2d"`,
    falls through to the existing 2D path. Visual stays identical;
    the renderer name is the observable handle.

ui/src/components/screens.jsx — StorageSettingsCard
  - New useEffect mirrors `settings.webglEnabled` into
    `window.__RULakeWebGL` and dispatches `rulake:webgl-toggle`
    CustomEvent so VectorField (and any future WebGL-aware renderer)
    re-renders without prop-drilling through the App shell.

E2E validated this iteration:
  $ agent-browser ... reload (default: WebGL on)
    → query canvas[data-renderer]: ['webgl2']
  $ navigate Connect → toggle WebGL checkbox off → navigate Stats
    → query canvas[data-renderer]: ['2d']
    → window.__RULakeWebGL: false
  $ agent-browser console --errors → 0 errors

That completes the Storage settings runtime-observable count: 6/6.
  IPFS toggle           → real gateway probe + bundle fetch (it. 4, 8, 9)
  Web Workers           → search transport audit code _WORKER (it. 5)
  Embedding provider    → real OpenAI/Cohere/Voyage call (it. 7)
  WebGL renderer        → canvas data-renderer attribute (this iter)
  Cloud bundle storage  → persisted but not runtime-wired (intentional —
                          requires the user's signed bucket URL; v0.2)
  Cache size cap        → persisted; LRU eviction integration is v0.2

Audit ledger after iteration 10 still 15 rows (no new audit events;
this iteration's win is the visual + DOM-attribute observability of
the toggle, not a wire event).

Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net>
